cards.front: What is the purpose of the toArray() method in Java?
cards.back: The toArray() method is used to convert a Collection object into a Array object.

cards.front: When to use the toArray() method?
cards.back: The toArray() method is used when you need to convert a Collection object into a fixed-size Array object for further processing or storage.

cards.front: Which JDBC objects support the toArray() method?
cards.back: The ResultSet and Statement objects support the toArray() method.

cards.front: What is the return type of the toArray() method?
cards.back: The toArray() method returns an Array object that contains the elements of the Collection object.

cards.front: Can the toArray() method be used with all types of Collection objects?
cards.back: No, the toArray() method can only be used with Collection objects that implement the List interface, such as ArrayList, LinkedList, etc.
